notes: Figma frame at canvas coords (4200, 5169); all child y values shown are LOCAL to the frame (0 = top of homepage). The very first visual band (y 0-1175) is the hero and is locked as reuse-as-is. The 'What we offer' 4-card strip (1:591, y=859) is inside the hero band and is already implemented inside hero-section.tsx, so the standalone WhatWeDoSection is no longer needed on this page. No stats band is present in this Figma, so ExperienceHarmonyStatsBand is removed.

sections.purpose: Top-of-page hero: site nav, eyebrow + 'Yoga for Self. Yoga for Teaching' headline, hero image + teacher card overlay, and the 4-card 'What we offer' strip just above the marquee.
sections.notes: DO NOT MODIFY. Includes site header (1:90), background image (1:33/1:35), big devanagari watermark (1:37 बोधि), eyebrow + headline (1:83), and 'what we offer' 4-card row (1:591). The standalone WhatWeDoSection on the current page becomes redundant because of this.

sections.purpose: Horizontal scrolling strip listing the eight limbs of yoga as a brand motif between the hero and the founder quote.

components.notes: Duplicated items in Figma (Yama, Niyama, Āsana, Prāṇāyāma appear twice) are just the marquee duplication trick — keep a single ordered array of 8 limbs and loop in CSS.

sections.purpose: Two-column band: founder quote with attribution on the left, supporting visual / portrait on the right.

props.quote: When a woman is empowered through yoga, her entire family, community, and future generations benefit.

image.alt: Acharya Ashok, founder of Bodhi School of Yoga

components.notes: Eyebrow uses text-mini tracking-wider tokens. Quote in text-h3 or h4 serif. Right column shows a portrait/photo block roughly 578px wide.

sections.purpose: Two-column 'Why Bodhi School of Yoga?' block: photo on the left, heading + intro paragraph + 'What We Stand For' bullet list + CTA on the right.

image.alt: Woman practicing yoga in a sunlit studio

props.heading: Why Bodhi School of Yoga?
props.intro: Founded in 2014 by Acharya Ashok, Bodhi School of Yoga was created with a simple yet powerful vision: to help people reconnect with themselves through yoga, wellness, and conscious living.
pillars: Making yoga accessible, inclusive, and empowering for every woman
Offering internationally aligned teacher training programs designed with women's unique journeys in mind
Creating a safe and supportive space for healing, leadership, and sisterhood
Helping women grow personally, professionally, and spiritually
Building a global community of empowered yoginis spreading holistic well-being

components.notes: Pillars rendered as a check-bulleted list. CTA is a text-link with arrow, not a filled button.

sections.purpose: Dark full-bleed band containing two stacked program rails: 'Yoga Teacher Training Courses' and 'Regular Yoga Courses', each with 3 program cards + a 'More Courses' link.

cards.title: 300 Hour Yoga Teacher Training Course — Online
300 Hour Yoga Teacher Training Course — Online

assets: 6 program card thumbnails (some duplicated across rails in Figma)

Reusable card. Image fills top of card, meta row + title + view-program link below.

items.caption: Ministry of Ayush Government of India

sections.purpose: Three testimonial cards side-by-side under 'A Path to Wellness / What our clients say'.

testimonials.quote: I came in to lose weight. I left able to teach a class — and a calmer person at home. The training is honest, and that's rare.
testimonials.authorMeta: TTC Cohort 11. Now teaching in Goa.

testimonials.quote: The back pain workshop did more in two days than two years of physiotherapy. I've recommended Bodhi to everyone I know.
I practice with Bodhi online from Berlin. Six in the morning, India time. It's the most consistent thing in my week.

components.notes: Content matches the data already in app/page.tsx exactly — no copy changes, only ensure visual styling (card padding, quote mark, avatar size/position) matches Figma.
Card has large opening/closing quote glyphs, avatar at bottom with name + meta.

sections.purpose: Dark full-bleed band containing (a) a final 'Begin where you are.' CTA block and (b) the full site footer with columns: brand, School links, Visit address, Stay close, plus a legal bar.

props.heading: Begin where you are.
props.body: Whether you want to teach, heal a specific thing, or simply move and breathe with people once a week

removed_sections.reason: The 4-card 'What we offer' strip (yoga teacher training / daily yoga classes / wellness workshops / Yoga Therapies) is part of the hero in Figma (node 1:591, y=859 inside the hero band) and is already implemented inside hero-section.tsx. removed_sections.action: remove from app/page.tsx (do not delete the component file)

uncertain.reason: Rectangle 161124039 is a full-bleed dark background that geometrically wraps the ProgramsGrid (607:8415). Treating them as one section, but it could also be intended to extend behind the FinalCta block — visually confirm via screenshot during QA.
Second rail's eyebrow text in Figma is 'Teacher Training' (same as first rail). Likely a copy-paste oversight by the designer. Recommend using 'Regular Yoga' (or similar) but flag for product review.

These two circular badges (128x128) appear pinned to the right edge of the accreditations card without captions, while the other 6 logos have captions in a 3-column rail. Layout intent unclear — possibly meant as 'highlighted' badges sitting outside the rail. Mirror Figma exact placement and confirm in QA.

Final 'Begin where you are' CTA is bundled inside the same Figma group as the footer (1:619). Splitting into FinalCtaSection + SiteFooterBlock keeps responsibilities clean, but they share the same dark background — make sure no seam appears between them when rendered.
